fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Massachusetts Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Massachusetts - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity massachusetts, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to massachusetts, speech-language pathologist compact massachusetts, massachusetts speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Registration of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Massachusetts.

license_types.title: Temporary SLP License
license_types.description: Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

steps.title: Complete Education Requirements
steps.description: Earn a master’s degree or doctorate in speech-language pathology from a program accredited by the Council on Academic Accreditation (CAA) in Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology.

steps.title: Complete Supervised Professional Experience
steps.description: Complete a supervised professional experience, which includes a minimum of 400 clock hours.

steps.title: Pass the National Examination and Apply
steps.description: Pass the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology and submit an application for licensure with the required fee to the Massachusetts Board of Registration of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology.

documents.detail: Massachusetts Criminal Offender Record Information check.

telepractice_text: Massachusetts permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. Massachusetts is not currently a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. A valid Massachusetts SLP license is required.
provisional_text: Massachusetts issues a Temporary SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: Massachusetts does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for full Massachusetts licensure.

faqs.question: How much does a Massachusetts SLP license cost?
Is Massachusetts part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
faqs.answer: No. Massachusetts has not enacted ASLP-IC legislation as of 2026.

faqs.question: Does Massachusetts allow telepractice for SLPs?
faqs.answer: Yes, with a valid Massachusetts license.

faqs.question: Does Massachusetts have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in Massachusetts. To work in Massachusetts, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Massachusetts?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 8 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Massachusetts while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Massachusetts does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for full Massachusetts licensure.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in Massachusetts
hero.dek: Massachusetts Speech-Language Pathologist licenses require a master's degree, passing the national exam, and completing a supervised professional experience.
